weekNoteHtml:
How to use this page. Three movement patterns per competition event. Pick the events you want to train, then do all three patterns for that event — or cherry-pick across events. Aim for 4–6 patterns total per session. Warm up first; stop a set short if form breaks down.

Tap any movement's teal header to open it full-screen — easy to read on the gym iPad, pinch to zoom, scroll through all three exercises.

This week — all-new movements. Strength & Power block opens: heavier loads, lower reps, longer rest, and the first explosive movements. Everything is fresh this week — read the cues, warm up thoroughly before the heavy sets, and stop at 3 sets. Progress with load or speed of the bar, never a fourth set.
testsLineHtml: Working number week. No formal test this week — tap your name below anytime to log a working number you want to track.

patterns.name: Comp-Pace Box Squat
patterns.rx: 3 × 6 reps · rest 2–3 min between sets · heavier load, drive off the box explosively
patterns.load: A KB 2 sizes HEAVIER than competition, at competition box height
cues: Sit to the box, then stand as fast as you can — make the bell feel light.
Take the full 2–3 minutes rest so every set is fresh and powerful.
Keep your weight on the box for a split second, then explode up.
Brace hard and keep your chest up — never let the heavy load round your back.

patterns.name: KB Goblet Box Jump-Squat
patterns.rx: 3 × 5 reps · rest 2 min between sets · squat to the box, then jump off the floor
patterns.load: A KB 1-2 sizes LIGHTER than your competition KB, held at the chest — speed first, load second
cues: Sit to the box, stand and leave the floor with a small fast jump.
Land soft with bent knees, reset your stance, then go again.
Use the lighter bell so every jump is quick — drop down another size if you slow down.
Keep the bell snug at your chest so it does not bounce on the landing.

patterns.name: KB Suitcase Squat
patterns.rx: 3 × 8 reps · rest 90 sec between sets · a KB in each hand at your sides, stand tall
patterns.load: Two KBs near your competition KB size, one in each hand
cues: Hold a bell in each hand and squat with your arms hanging straight down.
Keep your shoulders square and chest up — do not lean to one side.
Sit to your normal depth and drive up through your heels with intent.
Set the bells down to rest your grip if it gives out before your legs do.

patterns.name: Throw Technique Practice
patterns.rx: 3 × 3 throws · rest 90 sec between sets · 9 max-intent throws, full effort, full reset
patterns.load: Dynamax ball — 4 lb (women) / 8 lb (men)
cues: Hinge low and explode up, throwing the ball up and back over your head as far as you can on every rep.
Treat each of these as a real attempt — full power, then a complete reset before the next.
Drive everything from a violent hip snap; let your arms whip the ball overhead behind you.
Take the full 90 sec rest so each throw is fresh and fast — quality over quantity.

patterns.name: Trap-Bar Deadlift
patterns.rx: 3 × 6 reps · rest 2 min between sets
patterns.load: A trap bar loaded heavy enough that 6 reps is challenging but every rep stays clean
cues: Stand tall inside the bar, hinge down to grab the handles, then drive your feet through the floor and stand up fast.
Keep your back flat and your chest proud — push the floor away rather than pulling with your back.
Move each rep with intent on the way up, then lower under control and reset your setup.
Take the full 2 minutes rest between sets so you lift heavy and fresh.

patterns.name: Overhead Med-Ball Slam to Floor
patterns.rx: 3 × 6 reps · rest 90 sec between sets
patterns.load: A D-ball you can throw hard and overhead with control
cues: Reach the D-ball tall overhead, then drive it straight down to the floor in front of you as hard as you can using your whole body.
Fold at the hips and snap your stomach down into the slam — this trains the same explosive finish as your throw.
Squat down to pick the ball up safely each rep; don't bend over with a rounded back.
Move fast and explosively, but reset your stance before every slam.

patterns.name: Bench Volume @ % of Max Reps
patterns.rx: 3 sets · each set to ~50% of your max-rep test · rest 2 min between · press each rep up fast
patterns.load: Your competition bench weight
cues: Your app shows your personal rep target — keep it crisp and drive every rep up as fast as you can off the chest.
Take a full 2 minutes between sets so each set is fresh and explosive, not fatigued.
Stop the set the moment the bar speed drops or your hips lift off the bench.

patterns.name: Hands-Elevated Explosive Press
patterns.rx: 3 × 6 reps · rest 90 sec between sets · press up fast, hands stay on the bench
patterns.load: Bodyweight, hands on a knee-height bench or box — never airborne
cues: Set your hands on a sturdy bench in a push-up position, lower under control, then press up as fast as you can while your hands stay in contact with the bench.
Raise the bench higher to make it easier, or lower it for more load — keep both hands planted the whole time, no popping off.
Brace your stomach so your hips don't sag, and stop the set when the press speed fades.

patterns.name: Standing Band Chest Press
patterns.rx: 3 × 8 reps · rest 90 sec between sets · drive each rep fast
patterns.load: A resistance band anchored behind you, heavy enough that 8 fast reps is challenging
cues: Anchor a band at chest height behind you, hold the handles at your chest, and punch both arms forward fast to a full lockout.
Stand in a staggered stance and brace your stomach so you don't lean forward as you press.
Return slowly to your chest under the band's tension. Stop if the speed off the chest fades.

patterns.name: Max Hang Practice
patterns.rx: 3 × hold for time · rest 3 min between
patterns.load: Pull-up bar at ~80% of your current best hang time — heavier feel, longer rest, still sub-max
cues: Set up tight, shoulders packed, and treat each hold as a quality effort with full rest after.
Crush the bar and keep your fingers spread for maximum surface.
Breathe slow; step down deliberately when your grip begins to fail.
Log your best of the three holds for the app.

patterns.name: Weighted Hang
patterns.rx: 3 × hold for time · rest 2 min 30 sec between
patterns.load: Pull-up bar with a 5–15 lb dumbbell pinched between your feet or a light belt — pick a load you can hold 15–25 seconds
cues: Add a small weight to overload the grip, then hang active with shoulders packed.
Keep the weight steady between your feet so it does not swing or drop.
Grip hard and exhale slowly — this is a short, heavy effort.
Step down safely and set the weight aside the moment your hand gives.

patterns.name: Explosive Kettlebell Swing
patterns.rx: 3 × 8 reps · rest 2 min between sets
patterns.load: A kettlebell heavy enough that 8 fast swings is challenging — drive with the hips, not the arms
cues: Hinge at the hips and snap them forward to float the bell to chest height.
Let the grip ride the handle hard at the top — this trains a powerful, fast pull.
Keep your arms relaxed; the hips do the work, not the shoulders.
Reset your stance between reps so every swing is explosive and crisp.

patterns.name: Standing Slam Volume
patterns.rx: 3 × 8 reps · rest 90 sec between sets
patterns.load: Your competition D-ball, one size heavier
cues: Use a heavier ball this block — every slam is a max-power effort, not a volume grind.
Explode the ball straight down as fast as you can, then take your time to reset.
Drop to your comp ball if the heavier one slows your slam speed down.

patterns.name: D-Ball Floor-to-Shoulder
patterns.rx: 3 × 6 reps · rest 90 sec between sets
patterns.load: A heavy D-ball you can shoulder for 6 strong reps
cues: Squat down, hug the ball, then stand and drive it up onto one shoulder; alternate shoulders.
Power the lift with your legs and hips — explode out of the bottom.
Lower the ball under control to the floor; stop the set if your back rounds.

patterns.name: Kettlebell Swing
patterns.rx: 3 × 10 reps · rest 90 sec between sets
patterns.load: A heavy kettlebell you can hinge-and-snap for 10 powerful reps
cues: Hike the bell back, then snap your hips forward to float it to chest height — power from the hips, not the arms.
Keep your back flat and let the bell swing on a straight line.
This trains the explosive hip drive that launches your slam.

patterns.name: 60-Sec Pace Practice
patterns.rx: 3 × 60 sec of singles · rest 2 min between sets · fast, crisp turnover
patterns.load: Your jump rope, pushing a faster-than-comp turnover
cues: Spin the rope fast with quick wrists and meet it with quick, low feet.
Keep your bounce tiny so your feet are back on the floor in time for the fast rope.
Treat each set as a sharp effort and rest fully so the next set stays fast.

patterns.name: Heavy Loaded Calf Raise
patterns.rx: 3 × 14 reps · rest 2 min between sets · 1-sec pause at top and bottom
patterns.load: Heavy DB or KB in each hand — heavy enough that rep 14 is a real grind
cues: Push explosively onto the balls of your feet, then control the slow lower.
Pause at the bottom stretch so you can't bounce out of it.
Stand tall with a braced trunk so the heavy load stays over your feet.

patterns.name: Ankle Pop Hops
patterns.rx: 3 × 10 reps · rest 90 sec between sets · max height each pop
patterns.load: Bodyweight, explosive single bounces
cues: Pop straight up as high as you can using mostly a fast, stiff ankle snap.
Land softly on the balls of your feet and reset before the next pop.
Make each jump a fresh max effort rather than a continuous string of bounces.

patterns.name: Broad Jump — Distance Practice
patterns.rx: 3 × 4 jumps · rest 2 min between sets · near-max with a clean stick
patterns.load: Bodyweight
cues: Swing the arms back hard, snap the hips open, and jump forward for real distance — then stick both feet.
Drive through the floor and reach with the hips; absorb the landing by bending knees and hips together.
Fully reset at the line between each powerful jump. Stop the set if you can't stick the landing.

patterns.name: Low Box Step-Off to Stick
patterns.rx: 3 × 6 reps · rest 2 min between sets · max-quality landings
patterns.load: Bodyweight, stepping off a shin-height box
cues: Step off the low box and land on both feet at once, freezing the instant you touch down — like a gymnast sticking it.
Bend your knees and hips to absorb softly; aim for a silent landing with no extra hop or stumble.
Keep the box low and shin-height. Reset on top between reps and stop if you can't stick it cleanly.

patterns.name: DB Jump Shrug
patterns.rx: 3 × 5 reps · rest 2 min between sets · explosive, then reset
patterns.load: A moderate DB in each hand — light enough to move fast, around what you'd use for a hard 12-rep RDL
cues: Dip a few inches at the hips, then snap them open fast and shrug the shoulders up — come up tall onto the balls of your feet.
All the speed comes from snapping the hips, not from pulling with the arms — let the DBs hang.
Land flat and reset your stance fully before each rep. Keep every rep crisp; stop if they slow down.

patterns.name: 500m Practice
patterns.rx: 3 × 250m · rest 2–3 min fully between · faster than your 500m race pace
patterns.load: Rower · about 3–4 sec/500m FASTER than your goal 500m split, full recovery between
cues: These are short and fast — drive hard from the legs and make each stroke powerful.
Take full rest so every 250m is fresh and quick, not a fatigue grind.
Keep the chain straight and the finish crisp; stop a piece if your form falls apart.

patterns.name: Bent-Over Barbell Row
patterns.rx: 3 × 6 reps · rest 2 min between sets
patterns.load: A barbell you can move fast for 6 — lighten it to a couple of light DBs if the bent position bothers your back
cues: Hinge to about 45°, flat back, then pull the bar to your lower ribs with intent and control it down.
Move the bar fast on the way up — this is a power pull, not a slow grind.
Keep your neck long and back flat; if your lower back rounds, drop the load or switch to DBs.

patterns.name: Explosive Sled Row Pull
patterns.rx: 3 × 10 yd · rest 90 sec between rounds · pull fast hand-over-hand
patterns.load: Sled loaded light enough to move quickly — a couple of 25 lb plates
cues: Sit or stand braced, grab the rope, and pull the sled in fast hand-over-hand.
Snap each pull hard and reset quickly — keep the sled moving the whole round.
Keep a flat back and pull with the big back muscles, not just the arms.

events.tag: Strength & Power · explosive change of direction

patterns.name: Shuttle Pace Practice
patterns.rx: 3 × 150-yd shuttle (3 × 50 yd) · rest 3–4 min · fast and sharp turns
patterns.load: Bodyweight · run these noticeably quick — near your comp race pace
cues: Run three 50-yard lengths close to your target race pace, attacking each length with intent.
Brake under control into each turn, plant firmly, and explode back out — crisp but never a hard cut.
Full rest between sets keeps every rep fast and quality, capped at three.

patterns.name: Box Squat Drive
patterns.rx: 3 × 6 reps · sit to box, explode up · rest 2–3 min
patterns.load: Barbell or two dumbbells, ~50–70% of a weight you could lift once — heavy but moving fast
cues: Sit back to a knee-height box under control, pause, then stand up as explosively as you can.
Keep your back flat and drive through your heels — don't bounce off the box.
Lower reps and heavier load this week; rest fully so each set is powerful.

patterns.name: Wall Drive Sprint Start
patterns.rx: 3 × 10 yards from a leaning start · rest 90 sec · explosive first steps
patterns.load: Bodyweight
cues: Lean into a wall or rail, then drive out hard for the first 10 yards with powerful piston steps.
Pump the arms and stay low out of the start — this trains the quick acceleration your shuttle turns demand.
Reset fully between reps so each start is maximal.

patterns.name: Comp-Distance Sled Push
patterns.rx: 3 × 50 yards · rest 2.5 min between · strong and powerful (not timed)
patterns.load: Competition load + 10 lb per side (women: 2 × 35 lb / men: 2 × 55 lb) · overload over comp
cues: Heavier sled this block — push the full 50 yards with intent and full power, still sled work and not a timed test.
Body at about 45°, arms locked, pushing through your trunk — don't let your elbows bend under the heavier load.
Drive long, hard steps with full hip extension and rest fully between pushes so each one is strong.

patterns.name: Backward Sled Drag
patterns.rx: 3 × 25 yards · rest 2 min between sets · heavy, controlled
patterns.load: Your competition sled load or a touch heavier — heavy enough that 25 yards is hard
cues: Face the sled, hold the straps, and walk backward pulling it toward you — stay low with bent knees.
Drive through your whole foot and keep your chest up; this hammers the quads for sled strength.
Take short, controlled steps. Stop if your low back rounds or your footing gets unsure.

patterns.name: Loaded Step-Up
patterns.rx: 3 × 6 reps each leg · rest 2 min between · drive up powerfully, step down under control
patterns.load: A knee-height box with a DB in each hand — heavy enough that the 6th rep each leg is strong, both feet stay grounded
cues: Plant your whole foot on a knee-height box, then drive up tall through that heel until your hips are fully open — both feet stay in contact, never a jump.
Step back down one foot at a time under control; don't drop or hop off the box.
Lower the box or the dumbbells if your knee caves or your balance wavers, and keep every rep slow and stable.
